name: Circular gravel of the Sierra de la Demanda
name_original: Circular gravel de la sierra de la Demanda
description:
You have a day ahead of you to enjoy a mythical and spectacular Route, one of which once you try it, you need to return from time to time to repeat it.

The starting and ending point of the route is the town of Ezcaray, which can be easily reached by bus from Logroño. Surrounded by the mountains, it offers you the best culinary qualities endorsed by Michelin stars and a good variety of accommodation, from hostels to luxury hotels. It is a perfect base camp to tackle this Route on a weekend getaway to La Rioja.

The itinerary runs practically entirely along good tracks, some paths and a bit of road.

You will climb to more than 2000 meters high. So take spare warm clothes, even in summer, as a sudden storm can always form and the temperature drops considerably.

You will find water sources along the way; the food you will have to carry it from the beginning.

If it is hard for you, there is a good alternative to shorten the Route by returning to a beautiful road, the LR-415 from the top of Cruz de la Demanda, at kilometer 22.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Ezcaray features routes through the Sierra de la Demanda mountains, offering varied terrain for cyclists. The region is characterized by its mountainous landscapes, including significant elevation gains and descents. Many routes follow old greenways or traverse forested areas and river valleys. These paths provide a mix of surfaces suitable for gravel bikes, from compacted dirt to more rugged tracks. Best gravel bike trails around Ezcaray The most popular gravel bike trail is Circular gravel of the Sierra de la Demanda, a 40.7 miles (65.4 km) trail that takes 6 hours 49 minutes to complete. This route explores the mountainous terrain of the Sierra de la Demanda. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Mymerak gravel routes to the roof of La Rioja, a difficult 43.1 miles (69.4 km) path. This route ascends towards higher elevations, offering expansive views of the La Rioja region. Local gravel bikers also love the Alto de Pradilla – Ezcaray Greenway loop from Ezcaray, a 44.9 miles (72.2 km) trail leading through mountain passes and along greenways, often completed in about 6 hours 24 minutes. Gravel biking around Ezcaray is defined by mountainous ascents, greenway paths, and forested valleys. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels, with 16 routes available. The routes in Ezcaray are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 100 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Ezcaray's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many gravel bike trails are available in Ezcaray?
A: There are 16 gravel bike routes around Ezcaray, offering a variety of experiences through the Sierra de la Demanda mountains.

Q: What are the typical lengths and durations of gravel bike trails in Ezcaray?
A: The gravel bike trails in Ezcaray vary in length, with many routes ranging from approximately 30 to 45 miles (49 to 72 km). Durations typically range from 4.5 to 7 hours, depending on the route and your pace. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e750904904/circular-gravel-of-the-sierra-de-la-demanda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular gravel of the Sierra de la Demanda&lt;/a&gt; is about 40.7 miles (65.4 km) and takes nearly 7 hours.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of gravel biking in Ezcaray?
A: Gravel biking in Ezcaray is predominantly challenging, with 14 out of 16 routes rated as difficult. These routes feature significant elevation gains and descents through mountainous terrain. There are also 2 moderate routes available, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40016330" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ezcaray Greenway – Puente de Cihuri loop from Ezcaray&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a less strenuous option.

Q: Are there any circular gravel bike routes in Ezcaray?
A: Yes, many of the gravel routes in Ezcaray are designed as loops. Popular circular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e750904904/circular-gravel-of-the-sierra-de-la-demanda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular gravel of the Sierra de la Demanda&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23740657" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Pradilla – Ezcaray Greenway loop from Ezcaray&lt;/a&gt;, both offering extensive tours of the region.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on gravel bike trails around Ezcaray?
A: The terrain around Ezcaray is mountainous, characterized by significant ascents and descents. You'll encounter a mix of surfaces, from compacted dirt paths along old greenways to more rugged tracks through forested areas and river valleys. The routes often traverse the Sierra de la Demanda, providing varied and engaging landscapes.

Q: What do other gravel bikers say about the trails in Ezcaray?
A: The gravel bike trails in Ezcaray are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 10 reviews. Users often praise the challenging mountainous ascents, the scenic greenway paths, and the immersive experience of riding through forested valleys.

Q: Are there any easier gravel bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Ezcaray?
A: While most routes are rated difficult, there are a couple of moderate options that might be more suitable for beginners or families looking for a less intense ride. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40016330" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ezcaray Greenway – Puente de Cihuri loop from Ezcaray&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that follows a greenway, offering a smoother experience compared to the more challenging mountain trails.

Q: Where can I find challenging gravel bike routes in Ezcaray?
A: For advanced riders seeking a challenge, Ezcaray offers 14 difficult-rated routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050787372/mymerak-gravel-routes-to-the-roof-of-la-rioja" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mymerak gravel routes to the roof of La Rioja&lt;/a&gt; is a particularly demanding option, ascending towards higher elevations with expansive views. Another challenging ride is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31492179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Santa Bárbara – Cruz de la Demanda Pass loop from Ezcaray&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year for gravel biking in Ezcaray?
A: The best time for gravel biking in Ezcaray is typically during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can also be good, especially at higher elevations, but be prepared for warmer conditions. Winter riding is possible but may involve snow and colder temperatures, particularly in the Sierra de la Demanda.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest I can see while gravel biking in Ezcaray?
A: Yes, the routes often pass by significant natural landmarks. You might encounter mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3546522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de la Demanda Pass&lt;/a&gt; or enjoy views of peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4214289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Millán (2,131 m)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3427292" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Lorenzo&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also lead near mountain huts like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3266568" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bonicaparra Shelter&lt;/a&gt;, which can be points of interest.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or cafes along the gravel routes in Ezcaray?
A: While specific cafes directly on every trail are not guaranteed, Ezcaray itself offers amenities. For longer routes, it's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks. Some routes may pass through or near villages where you can find refreshments. Additionally, areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4052924" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bonicaparra Recreation Area&lt;/a&gt; might offer facilities.
tips:
It has been the end of a stage in the Vuelta a España on occasion.
Mythical port of the Sierra de la Demanda with privileged views over the valley and the mountain ranges.
Impressive viewpoint near the peak of La Rasilla, at an altitude of 1,874 meters. You can see the beautiful views of much of the Sierra de la Demanda.
Along with Cruz de la Demanda, it is one of the mountain passes at the head of the Oja Valley. Rise of the road bike classics.
An idyllic paved track under the beech forest, with fountains and streams where you can spot a bug cooling off in summer.
